nameMeanings.name: Jacob
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from Hebrew Yaakov. This was borne by perhaps the most important of all the patriarchs in the Book of Genesis. Jacob was the father of twelve sons, who gave their names to the twelve tribes of Israel. He was the son of Isaac and Rebecca and twin brother of Esau. According to the story in Genesis, he was the cunning younger twin, who persuaded his brother Esau to part with his right to his inheritance in exchange for a bowl of soup (‘a mess of pottage’). Later, he tricked his blind and dying father into blessing him in place of Esau. The derivation of the name has been much discussed. It is traditionally explained as being derived from Hebrew akev ‘heel’ and to have meant ‘heel grabber’, because when Jacob was born ‘his hand took hold of Esau's heel’ (Genesis 25:26). This is interpreted later in the Bible as ‘supplanter’ Esau himself remarks, ‘Is he not rightly named Jacob? for he has supplanted me these two times’ (Genesis 27:36). Jacob is especially common as a Jewish given name, although it also became very popular among the Puritans from the 16th century onwards, and has again been widely used since the 1990s. Compare James .

en.lifeSummary: Jacob Sunesen was born in Sjælland, Denmark as the son of Sune Ebbesen Hvide and Cecilie Ebbese Sverkersdatter. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Ukendt. He died on 17 May 1246, in Møn, Denmark.

fr.lifeSummary: Jacob Sunesen est né à Sjælland, Danemark, fils de Sune Ebbesen Hvide et Cecilie Ebbese Sverkersdatter. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Ukendt. Il est décédé le 17 mai 1246, à Møn, Danemark.

de.lifeSummary: Jacob Sunesen wurde in Sjælland, Dänemark, als Sohn von Sune Ebbesen Hvide und Cecilie Ebbese Sverkersdatter geboren. Er hatte mit Ukendt mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 17. Mai 1246 in Møn, Dänemark.

it.lifeSummary: Jacob Sunesen è nato il nel Sjælland, Danimarca come figlio di Sune Ebbesen Hvide e Cecilie Ebbese Sverkersdatter. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Ukendt. Morì il 17 maggio 1246, nel Møn, Danimarca.

ja.lifeSummary: Jacob SunesenはデンマークスジaeランドでSune Ebbesen HvideとCecilie Ebbese Sverkersdatterの息子として生まれた。彼はUkendtとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1246年5月17日にデンマークムoンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jacob Sunesen(은)는 덴마크 스jae랜드에서 Sune Ebbesen Hvide(와)과 Cecilie Ebbese Sverkersdatter의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Ukendt(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1246년 5월 17일 덴마크 o에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jacob Sunesen nasceu em Sjælland, Dinamarca filho de Sune Ebbesen Hvide e Cecilie Ebbese Sverkersdatter. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Ukendt. Ele faleceu em 17 de maio de 1246, em Møn, Dinamarca.

ru.lifeSummary: Jacob Sunesen родился в местности сджaeлланд, Дания. Его родителей звали Sune Ebbesen Hvide и Cecilie Ebbese Sverkersdatter. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Ukendt. Он умер 17 мая 1246 г. в местности мoн, Дания.

es.lifeSummary: Jacob Sunesen nació en Sjælland, Dinamarca como hijo de Sune Ebbesen Hvide y Cecilie Ebbese Sverkersdatter. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Ukendt. Murió el 17 de mayo de 1246, en Møn, Dinamarca.
